# Preface {.unnumbered}

Traditional business intelligence --- dashboards, static reports, and fixed queries --- is being replaced by AI that understands your questions, queries your data, and delivers answers in seconds.  This book accompanies a seven-session executive education program that gives professionals from any background a hands-on understanding of how agentic AI transforms analysis, reporting, and decision-making.  No coding experience is required.

Each chapter answers one question.

Chapter 1 introduces the tools.  You will install a coding agent, learn how the three major options differ, and understand the environments where AI can execute code.

Chapter 2 asks whether AI can analyze your data on the spot.  You will open a coding agent in a folder containing a spreadsheet and go from raw data to a written recommendation with charts in under ten minutes.

Chapter 3 asks whether AI can help you think through decisions.  You will use AI as a sparring partner --- challenging assumptions, running pre-mortems, and stress-testing your own analysis from Chapter 2.

Chapter 4 asks whether AI can connect to databases and let anyone on your team query them in plain English.  You will learn about MCP connectors, build a reusable "skill" that encodes a database schema, and watch AI write SQL for you.

Chapter 5 asks whether AI can go beyond answering questions and actually take action.  You will learn about agents, tool use, containers, and the path from a prototype on your laptop to a deployed tool on the corporate intranet.

Chapter 6 asks whether AI can answer questions about your company's documents without hallucinating.  You will learn about Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) --- how AI retrieves relevant passages, generates grounded answers with citations, and where it can go wrong.

Chapter 7 asks whether you can trust it.  You will validate AI outputs, red-team skills and RAG systems, and build the governance framework your organization needs before deploying any of this.

The answer to every question is yes --- with the right verification.

## Data files

The exercises in this book use two datasets.

The [Superstore CSV](data/superstore.csv) contains roughly 10,000 retail orders across four U.S. regions with columns for sales, profit, discount, product category, and customer segment.  It is used throughout Chapters 2, 3, and 7.

The [Chinook database](data/chinook.db) is a SQLite file representing a music retailer's operations with 11 related tables covering employees, customers, invoices, tracks, albums, artists, and genres.  It is the primary dataset for Chapter 4 and is revisited in Chapters 5 and 7.

Chapter 6 uses a [sample company handbook](data/company-handbook.md) for RAG exercises.

## Prerequisites

You need one of the three major coding agents installed on your machine.  Chapter 1 walks through the installation of Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and OpenAI Codex.  All three run in the terminal, read local files, and execute code.  No prior coding or data science experience is assumed.
